columns.name: body
sample: The Boston Celtics have clinched the best record in the NBA for the 2023-24 season with a 64-18 mark. Jayson Tatum averaged 26.9 points per game, leading the franchise to its best regular season in years.

LeBron James has cemented his status as the greatest scorer in NBA history, surpassing 39,000 career points. The Los Angeles Lakers forward now stands alone atop the all-time scoring list with 1,490 career games played.

Joel Embiid has been ruled out indefinitely due to a left knee injury. The Philadelphia 76ers are evaluating a timeline for his return, with Tobias Harris listed as day-to-day with right ankle soreness.

Luka Doncic has been sensational for the Mavericks this season, posting 33.9 points per game to lead the NBA in scoring. The Slovenian guard has logged several 40-point outings and continues to carry Dallas offensively.
Denver's Nikola Jokic has been a one-man triple-double threat, averaging 12.4 rebounds and 9.0 assists per game alongside his scoring. The three-time MVP continues to define Denver's title defense.
Trae Young has paced the NBA's assists race this season, averaging 10.8 assists per game for the Atlanta Hawks. His court vision and pick-and-roll mastery remain among the league's best.

The Los Angeles Lakers edged the Boston Celtics 120-117 in the marquee Christmas Day matchup. LeBron James led all scorers with 28 points while Anthony Davis hauled in 14 rebounds. Jayson Tatum scored 32 for Boston.
Jimmy Butler scored 24 points and Bam Adebayo grabbed 11 rebounds as the Miami Heat defeated the New York Knicks 108-102. Jalen Brunson led New York with 21 points and 8 assists.

range.min:
A long January road trip tested Milwaukee's depth and travel rhythm. The Bucks navigated multiple time zones and back-to-backs, with veterans leaning on familiar routines.

Players highlighted the value of scheduled film sessions on flights and shorter shootarounds during dense stretches. The training staff coordinated nutrition and sleep protocols across cities.

On the floor, the team experimented with smaller closing units, taking advantage of the low-pressure environment to test combinations they may want available in May.

It was a useful exercise that doubled as a meaningful step in the conference standings.
range.max:
Women's tournament Final Four storylines feature four programs reaching the women's biggest stage. Each program's roster reflects different construction philosophies.

Coaches around the country credit the developmental approach. The Final Four path requires patience and program building.

Front-office personnel and college coaches alike watch the matchups closely.

The patterns will sharpen across the weekend.

columns.name: readme
sample:
# Visual Studio Code - Open Source ("Code - OSS")

[![Feature Requests](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/microsoft/vscode/feature-request.svg)](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3Afeature-request+sort%3Areactions-%2B1-desc)
[![Bugs](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/microsoft/vscode/bug.svg)](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues?utf8=✓&q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+label%3Abug)

The `microsoft/vscode` repository is where we do development and there are many ways you can participate in the project, for example:

* [Submit bugs and feature requests](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues), and help us verify as they are checked in
* Review [source code changes](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pulls)
* Review the [documentation](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-docs) and make pull requests for anything from typos to new content

## Contributing

There are many ways in which you can participate in this project, for example:

* Submit bugs and feature requests and help us verify as they are checked in
* Review source code changes

If you are interested in fixing issues and contributing directly to the code base, please see the document [How to Contribute](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/How-to-Contribute).
# Linux kernel

There are several guides for kernel developers and users. These guides can be rendered in a number of formats, like HTML and PDF. Please read Documentation/admin-guide/README.rst first.

In order to build the documentation, use ``make htmldocs`` or ``make pdfdocs``. The formatted documentation can also be read online at:

    https://www.kernel.org/doc/html/latest/

There are various text files in the Documentation/ subdirectory, several of them using the Restructured Text markup notation.

Please read the Documentation/process/changes.rst file, as it contains the requirements for building and running the kernel, and information about the problems which may result by upgrading your kernel.
# TensorFlow

TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. It has a comprehensive, flexible ecosystem of tools, libraries, and community resources that lets researchers push the state-of-the-art in ML and developers easily build and deploy ML-powered applications.

## Install

See the [TensorFlow install guide](https://www.tensorflow.org/install) for the [pip package](https://www.tensorflow.org/install/pip), to [enable GPU support](https://www.tensorflow.org/install/gpu), use a [Docker container](https://www.tensorflow.org/install/docker), and [build from source](https://www.tensorflow.org/install/source).

To install the current release, which includes support for CUDA-enabled GPU cards:

```
$ pip install tensorflow
```
# React

React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces.

* **Declarative:** React makes it painless to create interactive UIs. Design simple views for each state in your application, and React will efficiently update and render just the right components when your data changes.
* **Component-Based:** Build encapsulated components that manage their own state, then compose them to make complex UIs.
* **Learn Once, Write Anywhere:** We don't make assumptions about the rest of your technology stack, so you can develop new features in React without rewriting existing code.

[Learn how to use React in your project](https://react.dev/learn).

## Installation

React has been designed for gradual adoption from the start, and **you can use as little or as much React as you need**:

```
npm install react react-dom
```
# Vue.js

[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/vue.svg)](https://npmjs.com/package/vue)

Vue (pronounced /vjuː/, like view) is a **progressive framework** for building user interfaces. Unlike other monolithic frameworks, Vue is designed from the ground up to be incrementally adoptable. The core library is focused on the view layer only, and is easy to pick up and integrate with other libraries or existing projects.

## Ecosystem

| Project | Status | Description |
|---------|--------|-------------|
| [vue-router] | [![vue-router-status]][vue-router-package] | Single-page application routing |
| [vuex] | [![vuex-status]][vuex-package] | Large-scale state management |
| [vue-cli] | [![vue-cli-status]][vue-cli-package] | Project scaffolding |
| [vue-loader] | [![vue-loader-status]][vue-loader-package] | Single File Component (`*.vue` file) loader for webpack |
# The Go Programming Language

Go is an open source programming language that makes it easy to build simple, reliable, and efficient software.

![Gopher image](https://golang.org/doc/gopher/fiveyears.jpg)
*Gopher image by [Renee French][rf], licensed under [Creative Commons 4.0 Attributions license][cc4-by].*

Our canonical Git repository is located at https://go.googlesource.com/go. There is a mirror of the repository at https://github.com/golang/go.

Unless otherwise noted, the Go source files are distributed under the BSD-style license found in the LICENSE file.
# The Rust Programming Language

This is the main source code repository for [Rust]. It contains the compiler, standard library, and documentation.

[Rust]: https://www.rust-lang.org

**Note: this README is for _users_ rather than _contributors_.**

## Quick Start

Read ["Installation"] from [The Book].

["Installation"]: https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/ch01-01-installation.html
[The Book]: https://doc.rust-lang.org/book/index.html
# Kubernetes

Kubernetes, also known as K8s, is an open source system for managing [containerized applications] across multiple hosts. It provides basic mechanisms for the deployment, maintenance, and scaling of applications.

Kubernetes builds upon a decade and a half of experience at Google running production workloads at scale using a system called [Borg], combined with best-of-breed ideas and practices from the community.

Kubernetes is hosted by the Cloud Native Computing Foundation ([CNCF]).
If you want to know more about CNCF, check out their [website](https://www.cncf.io/).

range.max:
# 🦜🔗 LangChain

⚡ Build context-aware reasoning applications ⚡

Looking for the JS/TS version? Check out [LangChain.js](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchainjs).

To help you ship LangChain apps to production faster, check out [LangSmith](https://smith.langchain.com).

[LangSmith](https://smith.langchain.com) is a unified developer platform for building, testing, and monitoring LLM applications. Fill out [this form](https://www.langchain.com/contact-sales) to speak with our sales team.

## Quick Install

```bash
pip install langchain
```

runtime: fix race in mgc when GOGC changes mid-cycle
docs: update rust usage examples in README
kube-scheduler: fix nominated node leak after preemption

columns.name: description
sample: Python is a dynamically typed programming language designed to be quick to learn, understand, and use.
JavaScript, often abbreviated as JS, is a high-level, dynamic programming language conforming to the ECMAScript specification.
TypeScript is a statically typed superset of JavaScript that adds optional static typing.
A subset of artificial intelligence (AI) that provides systems the ability to automatically learn and improve from experience.
Deep learning is a subfield of machine learning that uses neural networks with many layers.
React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces, maintained by Facebook.
Vue.js is an open-source model-view-viewmodel front end JavaScript framework.
Rust is a multi-paradigm, general-purpose programming language emphasizing performance, type safety, and concurrency.

columns.name: short_desc
sample: High-level, dynamic language for the web

columns.name: bio
sample: Creator of Linux and Git. If there is one...
Working on @reactjs. Co-author of Redux and Create React App.
Creator of @vuejs and @vitejs. Independent open source developer.
Creator of Ruby on Rails, co-owner of Basecamp, author of REWORK.
Prolific open source developer. Creator of Express, Koa, Mocha...
Systems performance and observability at Netflix.

range.max: Working on @reactjs. Co-author of Redux and Create React App.

variables.name: gallery_json
sample: [{"title": "Intelligent Code Editing", "desc": "VS Code offers rich code completion powered by IntelliSense, providing smart suggestions for variables, methods, and modules based on language semantics.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/copilot_code.png", "/static/images/feature_sections/accordion1.webp"]}, {"title": "Built-in Git Support", "desc": "Review diffs, stage files, make commits, push and pull from any hosted SCM service \u2014 all from within the editor.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/actions_workflow.webp", "/static/images/feature_sections/pillar1.webp"]}, {"title": "Extensions Marketplace", "desc": "Customize VS Code with thousands of extensions for languages, debuggers, themes, and developer tools.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/accordion2.webp", "/static/images/feature_sections/pillar2.webp"]}, {"title": "Integrated Terminal", "desc": "Use the terminal directly from VS Code, running shell commands from any location with full support for bash, zsh, fish, and more.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/codespaces_env.webp", "/static/images/feature_sections/accordion3.webp"]}]

[{"title": "The World's Largest Open Source Kernel", "desc": "The Linux kernel is the heart of billions of devices worldwide, from smartphones to supercomputers.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/pillar1.webp", "/static/images/feature_sections/accordion1.webp"]}, {"title": "Massive Contributor Community", "desc": "Thousands of developers worldwide contribute patches, bug fixes, and features to the Linux kernel every release cycle.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/accordion2.webp", "/static/images/feature_sections/actions_collaborate.webp"]}, {"title": "Subsystems Architecture", "desc": "Linux is organized into subsystems \u2014 networking, memory management, filesystems, drivers \u2014 each maintained by dedicated engineers.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/pillar2.webp", "/static/images/feature_sections/accordion3.webp"]}]

[{"title": "End-to-End Machine Learning", "desc": "TensorFlow provides a comprehensive, flexible ecosystem of tools and libraries for building and deploying ML models at scale.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/copilot_code.png", "/static/images/feature_sections/pillar1.webp"]}, {"title": "Keras High-Level API", "desc": "TensorFlow's Keras API makes it easy to build and train deep learning models with just a few lines of code.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/accordion1.webp", "/static/images/feature_sections/pillar2.webp"]}, {"title": "Production Deployment", "desc": "Deploy models to any environment \u2014 cloud, on-device, browser, or edge \u2014 with TensorFlow Serving and TensorFlow Lite.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/codespaces_env.webp", "/static/images/feature_sections/accordion2.webp"]}]

[{"title": "Component-Based Architecture", "desc": "Build encapsulated components that manage their own state, then compose them to make complex UIs.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/copilot_chat.webp", "/static/images/feature_sections/accordion1.webp"]}, {"title": "Declarative Views", "desc": "React makes it painless to create interactive UIs. Design simple views for each state and React efficiently updates and renders components.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/pillar1.webp", "/static/images/feature_sections/pillar2.webp"]}, {"title": "Learn Once, Write Anywhere", "desc": "React Native lets you build mobile apps using only JavaScript, while React DOM handles web rendering.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/accordion2.webp", "/static/images/feature_sections/accordion3.webp"]}]

[{"title": "Progressive Framework", "desc": "Vue is designed from the ground up to be incrementally adoptable. It scales between a library and a full-featured framework.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/copilot_code.png", "/static/images/feature_sections/accordion1.webp"]}, {"title": "Reactive Data Binding", "desc": "Vue's reactivity system automatically tracks dependencies and efficiently updates the DOM when data changes.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/pillar1.webp", "/static/images/feature_sections/pillar3.webp"]}, {"title": "Single File Components", "desc": "Vue's .vue files let you co-locate template, logic, and styles in a single file for better organization.", "images": ["/static/images/feature_sections/accordion2.webp", "/static/images/feature_sections/codespaces_env.webp"]}]

columns.name: description
sample: A state-of-the-art autoregressive language model for dialogue, instruction-following, and long-form writing. Instruction-tuned on high-quality preference data with RLHF.
Multilingual chat model supporting 20+ languages with strong code, math, and agent-style tool use capabilities.
Dense transformer language model trained on a carefully filtered mixture of web text, code, and academic corpora. Excellent zero-shot reasoning and chat.
Quantized build optimized for local inference via llama.cpp / Ollama. Preserves most benchmark performance at a fraction of the memory footprint.
Mixture-of-experts transformer with 8 experts per layer and sparse routing. High effective capacity at modest active parameter count.
Compact text-to-image model distilled from a larger teacher. Single-step sampling for real-time generation on consumer GPUs.
Latent diffusion model trained on a curated multi-billion image dataset. Supports classifier-free guidance and text-conditioned generation at up to 2048×2048.
Fine-tuned LoRA adapter specialized for anime-style illustration on a strong base model.

columns.name: readme
sample:
# huggingface/SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct

A state-of-the-art autoregressive language model for dialogue, instruction-following, and long-form writing. Instruction-tuned on high-quality preference data with RLHF.

## Model Details

### Model Description

This model was trained with a focus on quality, reliability, and ease of integration. It is released under the **MIT** license and is ready for drop-in use via the `Transformers` library.

- **Parameters:** 1.7B
- **Primary task:** Text Generation
- **Library:** Transformers
- **License:** MIT

## Intended Use

This model is intended for research, experimentation, and downstream fine-tuning. For production deployments, evaluate thoroughly on your target distribution.

## How to use

```python
from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained("huggingface/SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct")
model = AutoModel.from_pretrained("huggingface/SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct")
```

## Training Data

The training corpus was carefully curated to balance quality, diversity, and safety. See the accompanying paper for a detailed breakdown.

## Evaluation

Comprehensive benchmarks show strong performance across standard academic evaluations. Please refer to the Files and Versions tab for the full eval report.

## Bias, Risks, and Limitations

Like all machine learning systems, this model may reflect biases present in its training data. Users should evaluate outputs carefully before any production use and follow responsible-AI guidelines.

## Citation

```bibtex
@misc{huggingface_SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct,
  title  = {huggingface/SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct},
  author = {huggingface},
  year   = 2026,
  url    = {https://huggingface.co/huggingface/SmolLM2-1.7B-Instruct}
}
```

# meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct

Multilingual chat model supporting 20+ languages with strong code, math, and agent-style tool use capabilities.

## Model Details

### Model Description

This model was trained with a focus on quality, reliability, and ease of integration. It is released under the **MIT** license and is ready for drop-in use via the `Transformers` library.

- **Parameters:** 70B
- **Primary task:** Text Generation
- **Library:** Transformers
- **License:** MIT

## Intended Use

This model is intended for research, experimentation, and downstream fine-tuning. For production deployments, evaluate thoroughly on your target distribution.

## How to use

```python
from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained("meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct")
model = AutoModel.from_pretrained("meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct")
```

## Training Data

The training corpus was carefully curated to balance quality, diversity, and safety. See the accompanying paper for a detailed breakdown.

## Evaluation

Comprehensive benchmarks show strong performance across standard academic evaluations. Please refer to the Files and Versions tab for the full eval report.

For the license question — can I use the outputs commercially? The tag says OpenRAIL but the README says apache-2.0.
Bug report: tokenizer adds an extra BOS when calling `apply_chat_template`. PR incoming.
Has anyone fine-tuned this on legal / medical text? Curious about the LoRA rank settings that worked best.
I benchmarked MiniLM vs BGE-large on MTEB and found BGE wins on retrieval but MiniLM is 3x faster. Happy to share numbers.
Trying to run batch_size=4 on a 24GB consumer GPU. Getting OOM. Any quantization tips?

sample:
Indefinite integral:
  ∫ x² cos(2 x) dx = (1/4) ((2 x² - 1) sin(2 x) + 2 x cos(2 x)) + C
Equivalent expanded form:
  = (1/2) x² sin(2 x) + (1/2) x cos(2 x) - (1/4) sin(2 x) + C
Used by integration by parts (u = x², dv = cos(2x) dx) twice.

Pack 24 unit circles inside a larger circle of radius R.
Densest known packing:
  inner-circle radius r ≈ 0.176939 R
  container radius     ≈ 5.65 inner-radii
  filled fraction       ≈ 75.14 %, empty 24.86 %
Hexagonal packing:
  inner-circle radius r ≈ 0.169724 R
  container radius     ≈ 5.89 inner-radii
  filled fraction       ≈ 69.14 %, empty 30.86 %
Square packing:
  inner-circle radius r ≈ 0.163961 R
  container radius     ≈ 6.10 inner-radii
  filled fraction       ≈ 64.52 %, empty 35.48 %

Solve  y''(z) + sin(y(z)) = 0  (the pendulum equation)
  ODE classification: second-order, autonomous, nonlinear, conservative.
  First integral (energy):   (1/2) (y'(z))² - cos(y(z)) = E   (constant)
Differential equation solutions (Wolfram|Alpha):
  y(z) = -2 am((1/2) sqrt((c1+2) (z+c2)²) | 4/(c1+2))
  y(z) = +2 am((1/2) sqrt((c1+2) (z+c2)²) | 4/(c1+2))
where am(z|m) is the Jacobi amplitude function and c1, c2 are arbitrary constants.

Simplification — depressed quintic form (fewer items):
  x^5 - 20 x^4 + 163 x^3 - 676 x^2 + 1424 x - 1209
  =  (x - 4)^5 + 3 (x - 4)^3 + 7    [3 terms — "fewer items"]
Horner form:
  x (x (x ((x - 20) x + 163) - 676) + 1424) - 1209
Real root:    x ≈ 2.82987
Complex roots (each appearing twice):  x ≈ 3.7429 ± 1.8665 i

Spring pendulum (k=120 N/m, m=1 kg, l₀=0.12 m, l(0)=0.24 m, θ(0)=80°), after t = 6 s:
  final angle from vertical:  ≈ -73.26°  (= -1.279 rad)
  final spring length:        ≈ 25.21 cm  (= 0.2521 m, 9.925 in, 0.8271 ft)

4-cyanoindole  C9H6N2  (molar mass 142.16 g/mol)
12 lb = 5442.8 g  ->  ~38.29 mol
Mass percent:  C 76.04%,  H 4.25%,  N 19.71%

Oslo, Norway (59.91° N, 10.75° E) — predicted geomagnetic field on 20 June 2023
(Wolfram|Alpha uses WMM 2020, excluding local perturbations):
  Magnetic declination:                +4.51° E
  Magnetic dip / inclination:          +72.888°
  Total field strength F:              51.5 μT
  x component (north):                 +15.1 μT
  y component (east):                  +1.19 μT
  z component (down):                  +49.2 μT
  Horizontal component H = √(x²+y²):  ≈ 15.15 μT

columns.name: description
sample: Solve equations, factor polynomials, work with expressions and inequalities. Wolfram Alpha handles everything from simple linear equations to complex polynomial systems.
Compute derivatives, integrals, limits, series expansions, and much more. Wolfram Alpha delivers step-by-step solutions for calculus problems.
Statistical computations including distributions, regression, hypothesis testing, and data analysis. Input your data for instant statistical summaries.
Compute geometric properties of 2D shapes, 3D solids, and coordinate geometry. From basic areas to advanced differential geometry.
Explore prime factorization, divisibility, modular arithmetic, and number-theoretic functions.
Work with trigonometric functions, identities, inverse functions, and applications in triangles and oscillations.
Matrix operations, eigenvalues, eigenvectors, vector spaces, and linear transformations.
Compute probabilities, work with random variables, and analyze stochastic processes.

range.min: BMI calculations, caloric needs, nutritional analysis, and health metrics.
range.max: Work with trigonometric functions, identities, inverse functions, and applications in triangles and oscillations.

columns.name: examples
sample: [{"query": "integrate sin(x) dx", "type": "integral", "result": "\u2212cos(x) + C"}, {"query": "d/dx [x^3 * ln(x)]", "type": "derivative", "result": "x\u00b2(1 + 3 ln x)"}, {"query": "lim x->0 sin(x)/x", "type": "limit", "result": "1"}, {"query": "Taylor series e^x around x=0", "type": "series", "result": "1 + x + x\u00b2/2! + x\u00b3/3! + ..."}, {"query": "integrate x^2 from 0 to 3", "type": "definite", "result": "9"}]

[{"query": "mean {1, 4, 7, 2, 8, 3}", "type": "descriptive", "result": "4.17"}, {"query": "normal distribution mean=0 sd=1", "type": "distribution", "result": "PDF/CDF plot and properties"}, {"query": "linear regression (1,2),(2,3),(3,5),(4,4),(5,6)", "type": "regression", "result": "y = 0.9x + 1.1"}, {"query": "binomial distribution n=20 p=0.3", "type": "probability", "result": "Mean=6, Variance=4.2"}, {"query": "chi-squared test", "type": "hypothesis", "result": "Critical values and p-values"}]

[{"query": "area of ellipse a=5, b=3", "type": "2d", "result": "47.12 square units"}, {"query": "volume of cone r=4, h=9", "type": "3d", "result": "150.8 cubic units"}, {"query": "distance (1,2) to (4,6)", "type": "coordinate", "result": "5"}, {"query": "triangle with sides 3,4,5", "type": "triangle", "result": "Right triangle, area=6"}, {"query": "regular hexagon side 5", "type": "polygon", "result": "Area=64.95, Perimeter=30"}]

[{"query": "sin(pi/6)", "type": "value", "result": "1/2"}, {"query": "arctan(1)", "type": "inverse", "result": "\u03c0/4 = 45\u00b0"}, {"query": "simplify sin^2(x) + cos^2(x)", "type": "identity", "result": "1"}, {"query": "solve sin(x) = 0.5", "type": "equation", "result": "x = \u03c0/6 + 2\u03c0k or 5\u03c0/6 + 2\u03c0k"}, {"query": "law of cosines a=5,b=7,C=60\u00b0", "type": "triangle", "result": "c = 6.24"}]

[{"query": "probability of rolling 2 dice summing to 7", "type": "dice", "result": "6/36 = 1/6 \u2248 16.67%"}, {"query": "C(52,5)", "type": "combinations", "result": "2,598,960"}, {"query": "Poisson distribution lambda=3 k=5", "type": "distribution", "result": "P(X=5) \u2248 10.08%"}, {"query": "birthday problem 23 people", "type": "paradox", "result": "~50.7% chance of shared birthday"}, {"query": "P(A and B) given P(A)=0.4,P(B)=0.3,P(A|B)=0.5", "type": "conditional", "result": "P(A\u2229B) = 0.15"}]

range.min: [{"query": "1/3 + 1/4", "type": "fractions", "result": "7/12"}, {"query": "20% of 350", "type": "percentage", "result": "70"}, {"query": "sqrt(144)", "type": "arithmetic", "result": "12"}, {"query": "convert 2.5 cups to mL", "type": "cooking", "result": "591.47 mL"}, {"query": "how many days until Christmas", "type": "date", "result": "Computed from today's date"}]
range.max: [{"query": "weather New York City", "type": "current", "result": "Temperature, humidity, wind, conditions"}, {"query": "average temperature London in July", "type": "climate", "result": "22\u00b0C (72\u00b0F) average high"}, {"query": "coldest city in the world", "type": "extremes", "result": "Oymyakon, Russia: \u221267.7\u00b0C"}, {"query": "UV index today", "type": "uv", "result": "Real-time UV index for your location"}, {"query": "hurricane categories", "type": "scale", "result": "Saffir-Simpson scale: Cat 1\u20135"}]
